economic_notes: Cree Nation operates grocery store, gas bar, confectionery, restaurant, 1/2 interest in Pinesiw Energy LP, telecommunications cooperative, VLT lounge, 1/6 interest in Aseneskak Casino. Grand Rapids First Nation Development Trust holds restricted Manitoba Hydro settlement funds fair valued at $4,586,117. COVID-19 caused revenue reductions at Chus-Chug-U Gaming Trust VLT site. Hydro claim against Canada for damages related to development on traditional territory currently in arbitration.
notes: Going concern note due to high demand loan debt load of $34,888,016 and COVID-19 revenue reductions. Fiscal year is 2020 not 2024 as per document. Gain on disposal of $1,322 appears in prior year (2019) comparative only but listed in 2020 revenue lines in statement — treated as 2020 per statement listing. Four consolidated subsidiaries: Housing Authority, Gaming Commission, Grand Rapids First Nation Immediate Use Fund, Health Authority Inc. Six GBEs on modified equity basis including Development Corporation, Gaming Trust, Pinesiw Energy LP, Telecom Cooperative, Aseneskak Casino, Development Trust. Community Learning segment ($1,339,104 expenses) and Community Development segment ($8,089,762 expenses) noted but not mapped to standard categories. Provision of $1,109,758 recorded for guaranteed GBE debts. No schedule of remuneration provided. No budget figures presented.
